reasoning: Current yield as of May 9 is 5.15%, having spiked recently post-FOMC from 4.97% earlier in the month. High short-term mean-reversion suggests a probable decline back toward the ~5.0% range in the near term, meaning the effective yield is less likely to have increased by May 18. Moving into the medium and long term, we expect the Federal Reserve to cut rates from their current 4.25-4.50% range to around 3.6% by year-end 2025 (search_1_result_3). While this applies macroeconomic downward pressure on corporate yields, long-term asset price trajectories are subject to high macro uncertainty (e.g. proposed tariffs driving inflation), so following random walk dynamics for stationary yields, probabilities for more distant dates are pulled increasingly closer to 0.5 as uncertainty grows.
